text.title: flexibility-efficiency Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for flexibility-efficiency as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: No shortcuts: complex application where every action requires mouse navigation through nested menus.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Power users who perform the same actions hundreds of times per day are permanently blocked from efficiency gains. Adoption among expert users drops.

failure-mode.description: Expert-only interface: all options visible at all times, no progressive disclosure, new users overwhelmed on first use.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Novice abandonment rate is high when the cognitive load of first use exceeds the perceived value of the product. Onboarding completion drops.

failure-mode.description: No customization: fixed layout, cannot hide unused features, cannot pin frequently used actions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Power users with established workflows cannot optimize the interface for their repeating task patterns. Efficiency plateau is set by the default configuration.

failure-mode.description: Hidden accelerators: shortcuts exist but are not documented in menus, tooltips, or any discoverable location.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Undiscoverable shortcuts provide zero efficiency gain to users who have not memorized them from external documentation. The investment in building them is wasted.

failure-mode.description: Inconsistent shortcuts: Ctrl+D deletes in one part of the app but duplicates in another.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Inconsistent shortcuts violate user trust in the shortcut system; users stop using shortcuts entirely to avoid unexpected outcomes.

failure-mode.description: Agents generating shortcut tables often produce conflicts with Ctrl+W (close tab), Ctrl+T (new tab), Ctrl+P (print).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Common browser shortcuts are not always top-of-mind for agents; always run a conflict check pass before shipping.

failure-mode.description: "Multiple paths" generated by agents may silently diverge in behavior (e.g., double-click edits inline, right-click opens modal).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Behavioral divergence between paths fragments user mental models; flag these for human review before implementation.

failure-mode.description: Agents cannot observe actual shortcut adoption; efficiency gains claimed in an audit are hypotheses until measured.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Shortcut effectiveness must be validated through usage analytics; audit recommendations are feature hypotheses, not proven optimizations.

failure-mode.description: Human checkpoint required for irreversible bulk operations: agent can design the UX pattern, but confirmation dialogs and undo mechanisms must be verified by a developer.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Data loss risk is too high to implement bulk destructive operations without developer review of the confirmation flow and undo mechanism.
